summary: Patients with Werlhof’s disease—most likely misstated—are not associated with an established mechanism involving clotting disorders; thus, no clinically supported increase in bleeding risk exists during cardiac surgery based on current medical knowledge. The available literature provides no indication that such conditions contribute significantly to perioperative hemostatic compromise.
